CREATE TABLE test_list1 (id INTEGER, data LIST<INTEGER>);
CREATE TABLE test_list2 (id INTEGER, data LIST<INTEGER>);
SELECT data FROM test_list1 WHERE id = 1 || (SELECT data FROM test_list2 WHERE id = 1);
SELECT data FROM test_list1 WHERE id = 2 || (SELECT data FROM test_list2 WHERE id = 2);
SELECT data || (SELECT data FROM test_list2 WHERE id = 3) AS combined_data FROM test_list1 WHERE id = 3;
SELECT id, data || [11, 12] AS combined_data FROM test_list1;
SELECT (SELECT data FROM test_list1 WHERE id = 1) || (SELECT data FROM test_list2 WHERE id = 1) AS concatenated_lists;
SELECT id, (data || (SELECT data FROM test_list2 WHERE id = test_list1.id)) AS combined_data FROM test_list1 JOIN test_list2 ON test_list1.id = test_list2.id;
SELECT data || NULL AS combined_with_null FROM test_list1 WHERE id = 2;
